choice: v1: let the user enter the total annual tax-deferred contribution (employee deferral + employer match as one figure), keeping the data shape able to carry a match formula {match rate, cap % of salary} later. A match is just additional dollars into the same tax-deferred bucket at v1 fidelity — no distinct tax treatment — so a separate formula adds input burden without changing engine math.
rationale: b5a77b58 already routes deferrals into tax-deferred at target allocation; an employer match lands in the same bucket with the same treatment, so at v1's bracketed-net fidelity it's indistinguishable from a larger deferral. A formula matters for what-if precision but is deferrable; carrying the field keeps the door open. ProjectionLab/BoldIn expose a match formula — worth noting as a known later capability, not v1 default.
